steps.title: Square both sides
steps.content:
Since the right side must be nonnegative, valid solutions need $62-x\ge0$. Squaring gives
$k-x=(62-x)^2$

steps.title: Make one real solution
steps.content:
A quadratic has exactly one real solution when its discriminant is $0$.
$(-123)^2-4(1)(3{,}844-k)=4k-247$
Set this equal to $0$, giving $4k=247$. This produces $x=61.5$, which satisfies $62-x\ge0$. The answer is 247.
